stages.name: Reference Image Privacy Sanitization
policy_decisions: raw uploaded reference bytes stay private in artifact storage
JPEG and PNG references are decoded and re-encoded in worker before provider forwarding
WebP references fail closed before provider forwarding until safe metadata stripping is implemented
Mini App API upload path does not perform metadata re-encoding

checks_run.note: initial sandbox run could not access Go build cache; rerun with approval passed

stages.name: Provider Media Contract Registry
policy_decisions: video provider/model combinations are constrained by product-level media contracts before provider submit
real or unknown video providers without a contract fail closed before paid work
mock local development remains allowed and supports real-looking model codes through a wildcard mock capability
default video contracts allow one provider attempt and zero fallback attempts unless an explicit contract proves provider-side idempotency
video provider params are reduced to a safe product subset before adapter submit
provider metrics use bounded contract model_class when a video contract matches

stages.name: CPU-Safe Worker Fast Path
policy_decisions: provider video output is stored as a private original artifact before validation
ffprobe metadata is used as the cheap validation path when probe policy requires it
ffmpeg is skipped when provider contract, probe metadata, size limits and raw delivery policy prove the original video is delivery-ready
fallback transcode runs only when transcode policy allows it and the provider contract allows or requires transcode
unsafe provider video fails closed when neither fast path nor fallback transcode is allowed
delivery may use a raw provider original only when policy allows it and probe metadata marks it as safe mp4/h264
video fast-path metrics use bounded labels and curated model_class

stages.name: Backpressure, Concurrency And Cost Guard
policy_decisions: media queue backpressure uses Redis Streams consumer-group lag plus pending counts and never reads task payloads
new expensive media jobs are rejected before job persistence, reservation, outbox enqueue or provider submit when capacity is degraded
video active-job limits are repository-backed and checked before reservation
artifact upload concurrency is bounded before multipart parsing to protect API instance memory
probe, transcode and variant creation concurrency are per worker process resource guards
paid video provider fallback is conservative by default: one submit attempt and zero fallback submits unless config/contracts explicitly allow more
idempotent create retry returns the existing job before capacity guards run

stages.name: Image Reference Dedupe And Storage Lifecycle
policy_decisions: content hashes remain internal and are not used as public artifact identities
dedupe is allowed only for ready input reference images with same owner, same hash, same MIME type and same validation policy version
provider originals and output artifacts keep separate artifact ids and artifact-scoped storage keys
existing artifacts keep empty validation_policy_version so old uploads are not reused under new validation policy
cleanup is batched and backed by additive indexes for input references, lifecycle classes, job artifact arrays and deliveries
ready artifacts referenced by jobs or deliveries are not cleanup candidates

stages.name: Circuit Breakers And Provider Quality Scoring
policy_decisions: provider quality state is tracked per provider, curated model_class and modality
automatic provider/model disabling is config-gated and disabled by default
disabled providers are skipped only when another fallback candidate exists
if all candidates are disabled, worker keeps a degraded path instead of disabling the product completely
provider rate limits, provider errors, invalid media output, probe/transcode failures and provider success with product failure feed quality scoring
new metrics use bounded labels and never use raw prompts, raw user ids, job ids, artifact ids, private urls or raw provider payloads
delivery/capture gap is observed without provider/model labels because delivery workers do not own provider context

stages.name: Moderation And Safe User UX
policy_decisions: public media job error codes are provider-agnostic and bounded
Mini App upload failures return media_upload_invalid, media_upload_too_large or media_upload_unsupported
Mini App DTO maps legacy/internal media error codes to safe public classes before exposing jobs
worker stores safe terminal media failure codes while keeping detailed bounded product-failure reasons in metrics
terminal delivery failure releases the reservation and does not capture credits
VK media failure notices cover image and video placeholders and never include prompts, provider details or raw errors
frontend safe labels explicitly say credits were not charged for media failures without inferring provider/payment truth

stages.name: Production Observability And Load Readiness
policy_decisions: upload observability records only surface, result, reason, mime_class, bytes and cheap decoded pixel buckets
media queue backlog uses curated queue_class rather than raw payload data
worker policy decisions track accepted, rejected, degraded, skipped, fallback and kill_switch states without ids or prompts
provider waste has a media-specific bounded metric keyed by provider_class, model_class and reason
alerts include kill switch state guidance and money-risk guidance without exposing secrets or raw payloads
Grafana dashboards remain private and use bounded labels only

stages.name: Load, Chaos And Rollout Drill
policy_decisions: readiness drills use in-memory repositories, mock storage and mock providers only
concurrent small uploads stay bounded and expose only safe artifact DTOs
invalid upload floods reject before storage, job creation, reservation or provider work
duplicate same-user reference upload bursts reuse a policy-compatible artifact
rollout starts with dark-launch metrics and upload validation before provider contracts/probe/transcode
transcode remains disabled by default and fallback transcode must be one-model scoped if enabled
rollback relies on capacity degradation, transcode/raw-delivery kill switches and provider quality guard

stages.name: Final Production Audit
policy_decisions: reference uploads are behind MEDIA_REFERENCE_UPLOADS_ENABLED and default off in production
Mini App reference uploads accept only JPEG and PNG by default; WebP is disabled unless MEDIA_REFERENCE_WEBP_ENABLED is explicitly enabled
API rejects unsupported, invalid, oversized or excessive-pixel reference images before storage
frontend upload allowlist is aligned to JPG and PNG and remains UX-only, not a trust boundary
edge/proxy request body limits remain an operational rollout requirement outside this repository
VK Bot and Mini App still do not call providers, ffprobe or ffmpeg directly
billing capture and provider/media processing remain worker-owned and ledger-based
required_checks: docker compose config if compose changed
